expectations.text: Identifies the model as a GPAI model subject to Chapter V, and correctly applies the open-source exemption: free & open-source GPAI providers are exempt from the Art. 53(1)(a)-(b) technical documentation duties BUT must still comply with the copyright policy (Art. 53(1)(c)) and publish the public training-content summary (Art. 53(1)(d))
expectations.evidence: Table explicitly maps: '1. Technical documentation (Annex XI) — Exempted; 2. Downstream provider information (Annex XII) — Exempted; 3. Copyright compliance policy — Still required; 4. Public training content summary — Still required', matching Art. 53(1)(a)-(d) exactly.

expectations.text: Correctly assesses systemic risk: 8x10^23 FLOPs is below the 10^25 FLOPs presumption threshold (Art. 51), so no systemic-risk obligations (Art. 55) absent a Commission designation — and notes the open-source exemption does NOT apply to systemic-risk models
expectations.evidence: Response states model is 'roughly two orders of magnitude below that threshold, so your model is not presumed to have systemic risk' and explicitly notes: 'If your model is later designated as having systemic risk... the open-source exception falls away entirely and full Art. 53 + Art. 55 obligations apply'.

expectations.text: States GPAI obligations have applied since 2 August 2025
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'operative since 2 August 2025' and 'Your GPAI obligations have been in force since 2 August 2025 — this is not a future deadline for you, it is a current, live obligation.'

expectations.text: Describes the GPAI Code of Practice (published July 2025) as a voluntary compliance pathway with chapters on transparency, copyright, and safety/security, and explains adherence eases demonstrating compliance
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'Published 10 July 2025... Structured in three chapters: (i) Transparency, (ii) Copyright, (iii) Safety and Security... Signing the CoP gives you a presumption of conformity pathway with reduced ambiguity.'

expectations.text: Mentions AI Office enforcement powers over GPAI providers activating 2 August 2026 (fines possible from that date)
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'AI Office enforcement powers over GPAI providers activate 2 August 2026 — roughly one month from today. From that date, the AI Office gains full powers (information requests, model evaluations, fines) to check GPAI providers' compliance.'
